summary: codebuff-cli impersonates Codebuff and executes an unverified third-party binary
details: Package `codebuff-cli` ships a README copied verbatim from the legitimate Codebuff (CodebuffAI) project — including links to codebuff.com and CodebuffAI/codebuff — and registers the bin name `codebuff`, colliding with the official tool. The bin script `cli/bin/codebuff.cjs` downloads a platform-native binary from `https://github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/download/v<version>/codebuff-<platform>-<arch>`, chmods it 0755, and spawns it with the user's CLI arguments. The download host is a personal GitHub account (`Marcus-Mok-GH`) unrelated to CodebuffAI, no checksum or signature is verified, and the README gives users no indication this is a third-party rebuild. Combined, the impersonation README and the unverified third-party binary fetch form a typosquat-dropper pattern: anyone who installs this believing it to be the official CLI ends up running arbitrary code from a stranger's GitHub releases on every invocation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
